variablememory

Sequence Memory Test

Test your memory by remembering and repeating sequences. Watch the pattern, then click the tiles in the same order. How long can your sequence get?

1Level
1Length
0Score

Complete the game to see your score!

How to Play Sequence Memory

1

Watch the tiles light up in sequence. Remember the order.

2

Click the tiles in exactly the same order they lit up.

3

Each correct round adds one more tile to the sequence.

4

One wrong click ends the game. Try to beat your record!

Tips to Improve Your Score

  • Chunk the sequence into smaller groups of 2-3 tiles
  • Create a spatial pattern — imagine a path connecting the tiles
  • Say the tile positions out loud or in your head as they light up
  • Focus on accuracy rather than trying to memorize quickly
  • Practice regularly to build your sequence memory capacity

Frequently Asked Questions

What is sequence memory?

Sequence memory is the ability to remember the order of items or events. It's a key component of working memory and is essential for tasks like following instructions, remembering phone numbers, and learning procedures.

How does this test work?

Tiles light up in a sequence. Watch carefully, then click the tiles in the same order. Each correct round adds one more step to the sequence. The game continues until you make a mistake.

What's a good sequence length?

The average person can remember sequences of 5-7 items. Remembering 10+ items is excellent. With practice, you can extend your sequence memory significantly.

About the Sequence Memory

The Sequence Memory is one of our most popular brain training exercises. Designed to test and improve your cognitive abilities, this game provides instant feedback on your performance.

Regular practice with this type of cognitive exercise can help improve your mental processing speed, accuracy, and overall brain health. We recommend practicing for 10-15 minutes daily for the best results.

Your scores are saved locally on your device, allowing you to track your progress over time. Challenge yourself to beat your personal best with each attempt!
